title: Create execution roadmap artifacts
summary: Convert the rewrite plan into an execution-grade backlog that can drive autonomous delivery without additional planning passes.
deliverables: Root tickets.csv with unique ticket IDs, sequence, dependencies, status, success criteria, validation, instructions, and watch-outs; root PLAN.md with shared operating guidance and execution rules.
success_criteria: tickets.csv and PLAN.md both exist at repo root; ticket IDs are unique and sorted by sequence; dependencies are explicit and non-circular; exactly one next actionable ticket is marked ready.
validation: Confirm files exist; inspect ticket ordering; confirm roadmap ticket is marked done and next ticket is ready.
general_instructions: Keep roadmap stable unless new code facts require change; update status in place instead of forking new planning files; preserve one ticket per deliverable-sized unit of work.
watch_outs: Do not let roadmap drift from code reality; do not create circular dependencies; do not duplicate the archived TypeScript core into main.
files_or_modules: tickets.csv; PLAN.md

title: Create sanitized fixture, parity harness, and benchmark baseline
summary: Establish a deterministic, sanitized data and measurement baseline before deep refactors so later tickets can prove parity and performance instead of relying on intuition.
deliverables: Synthetic fixture config, rules, and SQLite ledger suitable for committed tests; repeatable benchmark commands for transaction list, summary, cashflow, and dashboard payloads; recorded baseline numbers and archive-to-main parity checklist.
success_criteria: Fixture contains no personal runtime data and is deterministic; warm and cold benchmark commands are documented and runnable; archived surfaces are mapped to Rust targets; baseline measurements are stored for later comparison.
validation: Run fixture-loading or fixture-generation tests; run benchmark commands at least once and record outputs; verify parity checklist covers dashboard, overview, and transactions surfaces.
general_instructions: Use synthetic data only; keep timestamps and IDs deterministic; separate API-handler latency from full-page render latency; prefer hyperfine or equivalent simple reproducible benchmarks.
watch_outs: Do not rely on FIN_HOME personal files; do not record noisy one-off measurements as gates; do not mix benchmark targets such as SQL time, handler time, and SSR time into one number.
files_or_modules: tests/fixtures; benches or scripts/benchmarks; package.json; PLAN.md

title: Extract shared read context and service seam
summary: Create a single Rust read-service seam that centralizes config loading, DB resolution, and read-only connection management for CLI, TUI, and future API consumers.
deliverables: A shared ReadContext or equivalent service module in fin-sdk; CLI and TUI read paths updated to use it; duplicated resolve_db style logic removed or reduced to thin adapters.
success_criteria: CLI and TUI both use the shared read seam for config and database access; read logic no longer depends on CLI presentation types; path precedence and read-only semantics remain correct.
validation: Run cargo check and cargo test; run representative read commands and launch TUI startup path; compare output behavior before and after refactor.
general_instructions: Keep the seam in fin-sdk rather than fin-cli; make it typed and presentation-free; preserve existing JSON and text behavior at the edges while removing duplication under the hood.
watch_outs: Readonly plus migrate currently has nuanced behavior; do not introduce circular dependencies between SDK and CLI; do not leak clap or serde_json specific types into the SDK core.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-sdk/src; crates/fin-cli/src/commands/view.rs; crates/fin-cli/src/commands/report.rs; crates/fin-cli/src/commands/sanitize.rs; crates/fin-tui/src/fetch.rs

title: Normalize envelope, discovery, and registry parity
summary: Unify the read-only contract across CLI and future API by fixing discovery gaps, registry drift, and envelope inconsistencies now instead of carrying them into fin-api.
deliverables: Shared or aligned envelope types; corrected tool registry metadata; explicit handling for version, tools, and health; output field definitions updated to match real payloads.
success_criteria: The registry accurately describes real read-only behavior; version, tools, and health are discoverable as intended; payload field names and outputFields no longer drift; envelope shape remains stable as ok/data/error/meta.
validation: Snapshot or golden-test fin tools --json; verify health and version payloads; verify existing automation can still parse JSON envelopes.
general_instructions: Preserve envelope keys and error semantics; treat the CLI contract as public; use this ticket to remove discovery ambiguity before API endpoints inherit it.
watch_outs: Do not silently rename established payload keys; do not expose interactive or mutating commands as read-only API candidates; do not let registry metadata differ from actual command outputs.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-cli/src/envelope.rs; crates/fin-cli/src/registry.rs; crates/fin-cli/src/commands/tools.rs; crates/fin-cli/src/commands/health.rs; crates/fin-cli/src/commands/version.rs; crates/fin-sdk/src/contracts.rs

title: Implement dashboard KPI and anomaly cashflow read models
summary: Port the archive dashboard's most useful cashflow semantics into Rust so KPI strips and anomaly indicators are computed once in the SDK rather than improvised in UI layers.
deliverables: Typed read models for last full month net, trailing average net, median spend, short-term trend state, rolling median expense, expense deviation ratio, and anomaly flagging.
success_criteria: SDK exposes typed cashflow/dashboard models with archive-equivalent semantics; current partial month is excluded correctly; anomaly metrics are test-covered and reusable by TUI, API, and web.
validation: Add unit tests against fixture data for normal, anomalous, and sparse series; verify partial month handling and sign conventions.
general_instructions: Compute these metrics in Rust services rather than in TUI or web loaders; document partial-month rules and trailing-window rules explicitly; keep naming stable and domain-specific.
watch_outs: Current month cutoff is easy to get wrong; income and expense sign conventions must remain explicit; avoid per-UI reimplementation of anomaly thresholds.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-sdk/src/reports.rs; crates/fin-sdk/src/queries.rs; new fin-sdk dashboard module if needed

title: Implement asset allocation and reserve composition read models
summary: Recover the archive allocation views by exposing typed composition data for available cash, expense reserve, tax reserve, emergency funds, savings, and investments where configuration allows it.
deliverables: Allocation models for personal and non-personal groups; reserve composition helpers; config-driven subtype mapping and percentage calculations suitable for both segmented bars and tables.
success_criteria: Group allocation data is exposed by Rust without UI-specific recomputation; personal and business or joint semantics are explicit; percentages and absolute amounts are both available and test-covered.
validation: Add fixture-backed tests for personal and business or joint groups; verify edge cases such as missing subtype metadata and negative available balances.
general_instructions: Use configuration and account metadata rather than hardcoded IDs wherever possible; return both raw amounts and precomputed shares so UIs do not re-derive business logic.
watch_outs: Missing subtype data, negative available values, and reserve month or tax assumptions can skew outputs; do not silently collapse different reserve semantics into one generic number.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-sdk/src/reports.rs; crates/fin-sdk/src/queries.rs; crates/fin-sdk/src/config

title: Implement historical balance and contribution series read models
summary: Bring back the archive historical time-series views by exposing merged all-account balance timelines, per-account balance series, and cumulative contribution series from Rust.
deliverables: Typed time-series models for all-account merged balances, per-account balances, and cumulative contributions; deterministic forward-fill and optional downsampling rules documented in Rust.
success_criteria: SDK can return all-account and per-account history without web-only helpers; forward-fill semantics are explicit and tested; contribution series exists for investment accounts and sparse dates are handled correctly.
validation: Add unit tests for forward fill, sparse series, and contribution aggregation; verify timeline ordering and date normalization against fixtures.
general_instructions: Keep date semantics UTC-normalized and deterministic; make any downsampling opt-in and reproducible; expose enough metadata for UI layers to render history without bespoke loaders.
watch_outs: Timezone drift, sparse series gaps, and hidden forward-fill assumptions can create false trend lines; do not make downsampling lossy by default.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-sdk/src/queries.rs; new fin-sdk timeseries module if needed

title: Implement expense hierarchy and flow matrix read models
summary: Recover the archive treemap and Sankey information in Rust-native form so both web and TUI can render category hierarchy and flow-of-funds views from the same source of truth.
deliverables: Typed hierarchical expense tree model; typed flow matrix or graph model with source, target, amount, and share fields; pruning and ordering rules for zero-value nodes.
success_criteria: Rust can generate archive-equivalent hierarchy and flow data; ordering and share calculations are deterministic; both graph-style and table-style UIs can consume the same model without post-processing.
validation: Add fixture-backed tests for hierarchy shape, zero-node pruning, ordering, and share totals; verify outputs for multiple groups.
general_instructions: Design these read models for both web charts and terminal tables; include amounts and percentages; keep hierarchy generation in SDK rather than web helpers.
watch_outs: Expense hierarchy naming collisions, transfer noise, and zero-value branches can make outputs misleading; do not tie the model to ECharts-specific fields.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-sdk/src/queries.rs; new fin-sdk hierarchy or flow module if needed

title: Implement runway projection and scenario read models
summary: Add forward-looking runway projection models so the restored overview surfaces can compare current-burn and minimum-burn scenarios with explicit threshold and breach information.
deliverables: Projection models for multiple burn scenarios, threshold and warning markers, projected balances by month, and derived breach month or runway summary metadata.
success_criteria: Historical runway and forward projection are clearly separated in the SDK; projection models support current-burn and minimum-burn cases; thresholds and breach metadata are explicit and tested.
validation: Add unit tests for positive, negative, and threshold-crossing scenarios; verify projection dates and burn assumptions are stable and documented.
general_instructions: Keep projection logic separate from historical report.runway semantics; make assumptions explicit in types and function names; prefer deterministic monthly projection steps.
watch_outs: It is easy to conflate historical runway series with forward projection; threshold units and months-to-breach semantics must be explicit; do not hide scenario assumptions in UI code.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-sdk/src/reports.rs; new fin-sdk projection module if needed

title: Implement paginated, sortable, group-scoped transaction read models
summary: Replace preview-style transaction access with robust read models that support stable sorting, cursor paging, counts, and row-detail payloads for both TUI and web.
deliverables: Transaction list API types with group filtering, sort columns and directions, stable cursor pagination, total counts, and selected-row detail structures.
success_criteria: Transactions can be queried by group with explicit sort and cursor semantics; counts and detail payloads are available; stable tie-breaking prevents duplicate or skipped rows across pages.
validation: Add integration tests for cursor pagination, sort ordering, and search edge cases on the fixture ledger; verify repeated page fetches are stable.
general_instructions: Prefer cursor pagination over offset pagination; include stable tie-breaks such as posted_at plus id; design payloads to support both table rendering and detail panes.
watch_outs: Pagination bugs are subtle and easy to miss; search semantics can drift from existing CLI behavior; do not force consumers to fetch the full dataset when they only need one page.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-sdk/src/queries.rs; crates/fin-sdk/src/reports.rs if summary metadata is shared

title: Batch-optimize accounts and ledger readers
summary: Remove the obvious N plus 1 patterns in accounts and ledger readers before any API wraps them so the system is fast by construction instead of by caching over slow SQL.
deliverables: Batched account balance and freshness queries; batched ledger plus postings load path; query count reduction and before or after benchmark notes.
success_criteria: view.accounts no longer performs per-account loops for balances and updates; view.ledger no longer performs one postings query per entry; measured warm-run performance improves on the fixture baseline.
validation: Run account and ledger benchmarks before and after; verify exact row ordering and payload shapes with tests.
general_instructions: Keep output semantics unchanged while reducing query count; prefer straightforward SQL over clever query builders; document any changed assumptions about ordering.
watch_outs: Performance refactors that alter ordering or null semantics are regressions; do not trade correctness for fewer queries.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-sdk/src/queries.rs

title: Refactor summary and report aggregation to reuse shared series
summary: Stop repeated full-series scans in summary-style reports by computing shared group cashflow inputs once and deriving runway, health, and reserves from reusable intermediate data.
deliverables: Shared aggregation helpers; summary and related report paths updated to reuse them; benchmark notes showing reduced redundant work.
success_criteria: report.summary no longer triggers avoidable repeated scans per group; summary-style outputs remain semantically identical; warm report timings improve measurably on the fixture baseline.
validation: Benchmark summary, cashflow, runway, and reserves paths before and after; run unit tests and any parity checks.
general_instructions: Prefer explicit reusable intermediate structures over hidden caching magic; keep business rules and trailing windows unchanged unless a ticket explicitly changes them.
watch_outs: Small semantic drifts in reserve or runway math are easy to miss during reuse refactors; do not regress archived KPI meaning in pursuit of speed.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-sdk/src/reports.rs; crates/fin-sdk/src/queries.rs

title: Optimize transaction list and search path and add storage support only if justified
summary: Profile and optimize the transaction query path for large ledgers, and add indexes or FTS only if benchmarks show the simpler path cannot meet the stated budget.
deliverables: Query-plan study; optimized transaction list query; optional schema migration for indexes or FTS if benchmark-gated; recorded warm and cold timings for list and search paths.
success_criteria: Warm paginated transaction list meets the target budget or an explicit justified exception is recorded; search path is measurably improved and documented; any DB changes are minimal, test-covered, and reversible.
validation: Run benchmarks and query-plan inspection against the fixture ledger; verify migration behavior if indexes or FTS are added; rerun transaction pagination tests.
general_instructions: Prefer the smallest storage change that satisfies the budget; keep list performance separate from substring search performance; document whether search is exact, LIKE-based, or FTS-based.
watch_outs: FTS adds schema and maintenance complexity; bundled SQLite features may differ across environments; do not add heavyweight storage features without benchmark proof.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-sdk/src/queries.rs; crates/fin-sdk/src/db/schema.rs; crates/fin-sdk/src/db/migrate.rs

title: Refactor TUI state model and context-aware cache
summary: Fix the TUI's current route-only cache and limited state model so richer group, sort, window, and route context can exist without stale or conflicting payloads.
deliverables: Context-aware cache keying; expanded fetch context covering group, sort, window, and route-specific state; migration of route refresh logic to the new model.
success_criteria: TUI cache entries are keyed by route plus relevant context; changing group or scope never reuses stale data; route state needed for later redesigns exists without ad hoc globals.
validation: Run TUI state tests and manual route switching flows; verify cache invalidation when group, scope, or filters change.
general_instructions: Keep the state model explicit and boring; make route context serializable or easy to fingerprint; prepare for later route redesigns without changing visible behavior more than necessary in this ticket.
watch_outs: Cache bugs are easy to hide during manual testing; do not bake route-specific assumptions into a so-called generic cache key; keep palette and key handling coherent while state grows.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-tui/src/app.rs; crates/fin-tui/src/cache.rs; crates/fin-tui/src/fetch.rs

title: Modularize TUI fetch and render architecture and add shared widgets
summary: Break up the monolithic TUI files into route and widget modules so later dashboard redesign tickets can land cleanly and stay testable.
deliverables: Modularized route renderers and fetch adapters; shared terminal widget helpers for bars, segmented compositions, trees, tables, and summary cards; simplified ui.rs entrypoint.
success_criteria: ui.rs and fetch.rs are no longer single monoliths; route modules compile cleanly; shared widgets exist for repeated visual patterns; later route tickets can change one route without destabilizing all others.
validation: Run cargo check and TUI tests; manually verify the pre-redesign TUI still renders after modularization.
general_instructions: Refactor structure before redesigning visuals; keep public behavior stable in this ticket; prefer small domain-specific widgets over overengineered abstractions.
watch_outs: Do not create a generic widget framework with no immediate need; do not mix route business logic into pure rendering helpers.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-tui/src/ui.rs; crates/fin-tui/src/fetch.rs; crates/fin-tui/src/routes.rs; new crates/fin-tui/src/ui or widgets modules

title: Redesign Summary route for dense KPI and allocation visibility
summary: Turn the Summary route into a true finance dashboard with a dense KPI strip, allocation bar, reserve coverage, and lightweight anomaly or watchlist signals instead of a sparse table plus gauges.
deliverables: New Summary route layout, including per-group KPI strip, segmented allocation or reserve bars, concise snapshot blocks, and any watchlist rows supported by SDK models.
success_criteria: Summary route surfaces runway, last-month net, trend state, median spend, net worth, available or reserve composition, and clear status cues without requiring users to visit text-only routes; layout remains legible on common terminal widths.
validation: Manual TUI validation at narrow, standard, and wide terminal widths; route-specific tests where feasible; compare content against SDK data and archived dashboard intent.
general_instructions: Optimize for information density, not ornamental borders; prefer one-line cards and segmented bars where they save space; keep labels explicit and tabular-friendly.
watch_outs: Overpacking the route can make it unreadable; do not rely on color alone for meaning; avoid hardcoded width assumptions that collapse below 100 to 120 columns.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-tui/src/app.rs; crates/fin-tui/src/fetch.rs; new summary route renderer modules; crates/fin-tui/src/theme.rs

title: Redesign Cashflow route around grouped monthly bars and anomaly cues
summary: Replace weak line-like encodings with terminal-native grouped or diverging monthly bars, explicit deviation markers, and compact trailing metrics for income, expense, and net.
deliverables: A cashflow route that uses grouped monthly bars or centered diverging bars, explicit deviation and savings indicators, and a compact reserve or runway footer.
success_criteria: Income, expense, and net are easy to compare month by month in the terminal; anomaly or deviation state is visible without reading raw numbers only; color usage is consistent and secondary to labels and sign cues.
validation: Manual validation on real terminal sizes; compare route numbers to SDK models; add helper tests for bar scaling or label formatting where practical.
general_instructions: Use consistent color families such as lighter blue for income and darker blue for expense if a single-family palette is chosen; prefer grouped bars over overlapping bars unless overlap readability is proven.
watch_outs: Line-chart nostalgia is a trap in terminal UIs; do not compress labels until bars become meaningless; preserve sign clarity and explicit units.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-tui/src/fetch.rs; new cashflow route modules; shared widget helpers; crates/fin-tui/src/theme.rs

title: Redesign Overview route for account drilldown, history, and projection
summary: Upgrade the Overview route from a static balance bar chart into a split view that shows account inventory on one side and selected-account history or projection detail on the other.
deliverables: Overview layout with account list or table, selected-account focus state, historical balance panel, contribution-aware investment view where applicable, and retained freshness visibility.
success_criteria: Users can inspect total context and then drill into one account without leaving the route; history and projections are visible in terminal-friendly form; stale account data remains visible and actionable.
validation: Manual validation of selection behavior and layout at different widths; compare values to SDK history and projection models; add tests for state transitions where possible.
general_instructions: Preserve keyboard-only navigation; keep selection obvious; make the route useful for both regular cash accounts and contribution-based investment accounts.
watch_outs: Do not continue using absolute-value bars as the primary explanation of account state; avoid requiring a mouse or hidden key chords for drilldown.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-tui/src/app.rs; crates/fin-tui/src/fetch.rs; new overview route modules

title: Redesign Categories route for pareto, hierarchy, and flow-of-funds insight
summary: Make Categories a high-signal analytical route by combining pareto data, expense hierarchy, leakage, and flow-of-funds views instead of limiting it to a chart and a sparkline table.
deliverables: Categories route with top categories, indented expense tree, uncategorized leakage summary, and flow matrix or linked flow summary supported by SDK hierarchy and flow models.
success_criteria: Categories route surfaces both concentration and structure; users can see top spend, category hierarchy, leakage, and where money flows; information density improves without becoming visually chaotic.
validation: Manual route validation on multiple widths; compare totals and percentages to SDK models; add focused tests for tree or matrix formatting helpers where practical.
general_instructions: Use trees and tables where they communicate structure better than faux charts; preserve the leakage cue; order sections by likely diagnostic value.
watch_outs: Hierarchy rendering can explode vertically if not pruned or capped; do not recreate a treemap badly in text when an indented tree would be clearer.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-tui/src/fetch.rs; new categories route modules; shared tree and matrix widgets

title: Redesign Transactions route for group, sort, paging, and detail inspection
summary: Turn Transactions into a high-volume inspection tool with group filters, stable sorting, cursor paging, and a selected-row detail pane instead of a global preview table.
deliverables: Transactions route with group selection, sort controls, cursor paging, search, range summary, and row-detail panel sourced from the new SDK transaction models.
success_criteria: Thousands of transactions are navigable without loading or rendering everything at once; group scope and sort state are visible; selected-row details provide more context than a single table row.
validation: Manual validation on large fixture datasets; verify page boundaries, sorting, and search behavior; add tests for cursor and selection state transitions.
general_instructions: Prefer server or SDK pagination to local full-list filtering when volume grows; keep the search affordance discoverable; ensure the detail pane does not starve the main table of width.

files_or_modules: crates/fin-tui/src/app.rs; crates/fin-tui/src/fetch.rs; new transactions route modules

summary: Finish the TUI redesign by removing the leftover plain-text report dump as the default analytical escape hatch and bringing the palette, footer hints, and route model up to date.
deliverables: Structured replacement for Reports route or an intentional route removal with equivalent structured surfaces elsewhere; updated palette actions, footer hints, and route labels or IDs.
success_criteria: No major analytical route remains plain-text by accident; palette actions expose current route controls cleanly; footer hints match real keybindings and state model.
validation: Manual verification of navigation, palette actions, and route availability; run TUI tests and cargo check.
general_instructions: Be explicit if Reports is removed or folded into another route; keep command-palette behavior aligned with actual route context and new filters.
watch_outs: Do not delete useful metrics without providing a structured replacement; do not let palette metadata and real behavior drift apart again.

title: Scaffold fin-api crate and workspace integration
summary: Create the new fin-api crate as a thin Rust daemon that depends on fin-sdk and is wired into the workspace, build, and test surfaces without introducing duplicate domain logic.
deliverables: New crates/fin-api package, Cargo manifest, binary entrypoint, workspace membership, and minimal boot path that compiles and runs.
success_criteria: fin-api builds as part of the workspace; it depends on fin-sdk rather than fin-cli; no business logic is duplicated into the daemon scaffold; root cargo and build scripts are aware of the crate where needed.
validation: Run cargo check and cargo test for the workspace; confirm the new binary starts and exits cleanly in placeholder form.
general_instructions: Keep fin-api thin from day one; prefer direct SDK service calls over CLI command reuse; keep startup configuration explicit and local-first.
watch_outs: Do not route API requests through fin-cli subprocesses; do not move domain logic out of fin-sdk into the daemon.
files_or_modules: Cargo.toml; crates/fin-api/Cargo.toml; crates/fin-api/src/main.rs

title: Implement server runtime, Unix socket default transport, and lifecycle management
summary: Build the local daemon runtime around HTTP plus JSON over a Unix domain socket by default, with loopback TCP fallback only where portability or debugging requires it.
deliverables: HTTP server runtime, socket path configuration, stale socket cleanup, graceful shutdown, readiness handling, optional loopback TCP fallback, and structured stderr logging.
success_criteria: fin-api serves over a Unix socket by default; stale sockets are cleaned up safely; readiness and shutdown are reliable; a TCP fallback exists without changing the API surface.
validation: Start the daemon, hit a probe endpoint over the Unix socket, restart it to verify stale-socket cleanup, and confirm clean shutdown behavior.
general_instructions: Treat this as a local daemon first; keep logs quiet by default but explicit on failure; separate transport concerns from handler logic.
watch_outs: Socket path length and permissions can fail in surprising ways; do not assume Unix sockets work everywhere; keep fallback transport optional rather than primary.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-api/src/main.rs; new crates/fin-api/src/server modules; package or script hooks if needed

success_criteria: API orientation endpoints return the same contract shape as the CLI; health, tools, and version are complete and accurate; error codes and hints are stable and actionable.
validation: Add API contract tests for orientation endpoints; compare outputs to CLI expectations where applicable.
general_instructions: Reuse the corrected discovery and envelope contract from SDK-002; keep the handlers thin and typed; measure elapsed time consistently.
watch_outs: Do not create a second inconsistent discovery system; do not expose internal panic or database details in error messages.
files_or_modules: crates/fin-api/src/main.rs; new crates/fin-api/src/handlers; crates/fin-sdk/src/contracts.rs; crates/fin-cli/src/registry.rs

summary: Finish the daemon properly by adding contract coverage, benchmark coverage, and operator-facing usage documentation so fin-api is a stable product surface rather than an internal experiment.
deliverables: API integration tests, contract snapshots, benchmark scripts or commands, and concise operator docs covering startup, transport, health checks, and troubleshooting.

summary: Bring the SvelteKit package back onto main with its tooling and structure intact while ensuring it is positioned as a pure API client rather than a second application core.
